description: Order-independent map equality (§59.9 / §45). @a is built by inserting X then Y; @b by inserting Y then X — the SAME entries in a DIFFERENT insertion order. The derived @a == @b is TRUE: map equality is structural and order-independent (two maps are equal iff they have the same key->value entries, regardless of insertion/iteration order). Observed via the impl-neutral derived boolean + DOM.
rationale: §59.9 @m1 == @m2 is structural and order-independent; §45 reuses the structural == path. Same entries inserted in opposite order compare equal.
